name: Example
description: Flows to illustrate different structures and operations you may want to use in your own flows.

entries.name: Add Tab to Google Sheet
entries.description: Takes an array of arrays or an array of objects and creates a new tab in a Google Sheet having the given name and containing the given data under the account for the given Google authKey

entries.name: Ansible Faas Example
entries.description: This workflow provides an Ansible FaaS example. It uses the Ansible Builder block and Ansible Pull block. The pull block is used to pull a remote copy of Ansible on each managed node, run through cron and update playbook source using a repository.

entries.name: Example 02 Call_Hello_World
entries.description: This flow provides an example of how one Pliant workflow can call another. We call the "Hello_World" workflow here, and that workflow is represented as another action block in the workflow.

entries.name: Example 03 Arrays
entries.description:
This workflow shows an example of a few common array functions and how arrays can be used and manipulated in Pliant

ArrayReverse
ArrayJoin
ForEach

Arrays are used anywhere you have a batch or set to work with

entries.name: Example 04 Objects
entries.description:
This flow is to illustrate how objects can be created, referenced, and manipulated in Pliant

Objects are represented using JSON syntax. An object can have multiple named properties, and each property can be a value of any type, including an array or another object. 

In this flow, we consider an example object as a table (furniture, not database) and its properties

entries.name: Example 05 If_Then
entries.description:
This flow illustrates the use of If..Then statements in Pliant, along with some expression syntax.

If..Then statements are for when your workflow needs to make a decision while it is executing

We will use an array to capture data as this flow executes, and use If..Then blocks to determine what strings we push into the array

entries.name: Example 06 Exit and Throw
entries.description:
There are two blocks you can use to make a flow complete before it reaches the end: Exit and Throw 

Set different values for the "exit_status" variable and see what happens (be sure to try 0)

This is useful if you have a way that your flow can finish early, or if you want it to stop working on a serious error.

entries.name: Example 07 While Loop
entries.description:
A while loop will execute until a given condition evaluates to false

Use these when you do not know how many times you need to execute a loop, but you know when you would like it to stop.

entries.name: Example 08 Loop Interruption with Break and Continue
entries.path: Example 08 Loop Interruption with Break and Continue.zip
entries.description:
The action blocks "Break" and "Continue" are used to control the flow of While and ForEach loops. 
 Continue will skip ahead to the next loop iteration.  
 Break will exit the loop.

entries.name: Example 09 Try_Catch
entries.description: Things can co wrong in any kind of program, even in the low-code world. A Try-Catch can help you handle those unexpected exceptions

entries.name: Example 10 Sleep
entries.description:
Sleep block and time functions - when your flows need to wait

We also have a number of Data/Time functions available

Also illustrates how to use a while loop that will time out after a certain amount of time

entries.name: Example 11 Switch Case
entries.description:
How to make a decision when there are more than two choices.
The SwitchCase block provides a way to compare a given expression against a number of other expressions, and provides a default path for when there is no match.

entries.name: Generate Random Character String
entries.description:
Generates a string of random characters in a given length
Uses Common blocks generate bytes, convert to text, then cut to length.

entries.name: Hello Ansible FaaS
entries.description: Example how to use Ansible Playbook from FaaS with a parameter

entries.name: Iterate object keys
entries.description: This flow demonstrates how you can iterate through object keys and object values

entries.name: JavaScript Function Example
entries.description:
Shows how to use the Function block within a workflow to run Vanilla JS. 
Starts with an array of objects and returns a count of objects that match a certain criteria

entries.name: Loop timeout demo
entries.description: A flow to illustrate how a user can build logic around a loop in Pliant to insure that it will not run forever. The loop will be broken if it runs for more than the maximum number of seconds.

entries.name: Next Element Example
entries.description:
Demonstrate accessing the next element in the foreach array
Handle the case where there is no next element gracefully
Does not return a result. Watch the status message area in the Logs panel to see the action.

entries.name: Set role members in Concert Workflows
entries.path: Set role members in Concert Workflows.zip
entries.description: Replaces the user membership of a CW role while preserving all other role properties (policies, worker groups, description). Fetches the current role state before updating to avoid overwriting unrelated fields.

entries.name: Ultimate Excel Example
entries.description:
Take an array of objects filled with dummy data, populates an Excel sheet, then shows examples of putting the sheet:
*onto a Linux server via SFTP
*onto a Windows server via Samba
*into AWS S3
*in an email as an attachment
